checks.a_analysis: The converted HTML faithfully maintains the visual organization and flow of the original document. The vendor-specific sections (Broadcom, Sophos, Trellix, CrowdStrike, Cisco) appear in the correct sequence. While the PDF is fixed-page and the HTML is a single continuous scroll, the information is organized in the same relative positions, ensuring that a user can find the same information easily. The multi-column table layouts from the PDF are preserved as structured HTML tables.

All tables in the document, including the complex pricing grids for CrowdStrike (Attachment A) and Cisco (Attachment A), are rendered correctly in the HTML. Table headers are distinct, rows and columns are properly aligned, and all data remains legible. No cells are overlapping, and the structure of the tiered pricing tables (Good/Better/Best for Sophos) is maintained correctly.

The source PDF is primarily text and table-based. There are no meaningful images (such as charts, diagrams, or photos) in the source document that are missing from the HTML output. Decorative font choices for section titles are appropriately rendered as text headings.

A thorough visual inspection confirms there is no duplication of body content. While section headings (e.g., 'Professional Services') appear multiple times, this is consistent with the source document where such sections are repeated for different vendors. No individual tables or paragraphs of body text are repeated incorrectly.
